basics.name: Dr. Lena Vasquez
basics.label: Staff Software Engineer, Distributed Systems
basics.summary: Staff engineer with 14 years building large-scale distributed storage and streaming systems. I lead cross-team technical direction, mentor senior engineers, and still spend most of my week in the codebase. My work spans consensus protocols, multi-region data replication, and the boring-but-critical operability that keeps petabyte-scale systems healthy.

work.name: Confluent
work.description: Data streaming platform built on Apache Kafka
work.summary: Technical lead for the multi-region replication and tiered-storage initiatives.
highlights: Designed the tiered-storage architecture that offloaded 80% of broker disk usage to object stores
Led a six-engineer working group to ship active-active cluster linking across three cloud regions
Cut p99 produce latency 40% by reworking the replication fetch path
Authored the internal design-review rubric now used org-wide

work.name: Dropbox
work.summary: Worked on Magic Pocket, Dropbox's exabyte-scale custom storage system.
highlights: Built the erasure-coding pipeline that reduced storage cost per byte by 25%
Owned the cross-zone repair scheduler responsible for durability SLAs
Mentored four engineers, two of whom were promoted to senior

work.name: Rackspace
work.summary: Contributed to OpenStack Swift, the open-source object storage project.
highlights: Implemented container-sync replication improvements upstreamed to OpenStack Swift
Reduced cluster rebalance time by redesigning the ring-builder partition assignment

education.score: Dissertation: Consistency Trade-offs in Geo-Replicated Key-Value Stores

publications.name: Bounded Staleness for Geo-Replicated Key-Value Stores
publications.summary: A tunable consistency model that bounds replica staleness while preserving low write latency across regions.

publications.name: Operating Tiered Storage at Streaming Scale
publications.summary: Lessons from offloading streaming log segments to object storage without sacrificing read latency.

awards.title: Best Paper Award
awards.summary: Recognized for the geo-replication consistency model paper.

awards.title: Distinguished Engineer Spotlight
awards.summary: Internal recognition for technical leadership on the tiered-storage launch.

projects.name: raft-lab
projects.description: A teaching implementation of the Raft consensus protocol with a deterministic network simulator.
highlights: Used in a graduate distributed-systems course at two universities
Includes a fault-injection harness for partition and clock-skew scenarios

references.name: Marcus Feng, Engineering Director at Confluent
references.reference: Lena is the engineer other staff engineers go to. She has a rare ability to hold an entire distributed system in her head and still write the cleanest pull request on the team. Her design reviews raised the bar for the whole org.

references.name: Dr. Anita Bose, Professor of Computer Science, UT Austin
references.reference: Lena was among the strongest doctoral students I have advised. Her OSDI paper continues to be cited, and she has the unusual gift of making hard systems ideas legible to students.
